calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel Michelle Elison and Open Door Legal, counsel of record for Defendant Robert A. Johnson. The motion is GRANTED.

The court has carefully reviewed counsel's motion and declaration and Defendant Johnson's opposition, including his attachments. It is clear the attorney-client relationship is broken. Counsel's motion is not for an improper purpose and the requested relief is supported by the evidence and will not cause undue prejudice. Good cause appearing, the motion is granted.

No later than 8:30 am on 3/20/2025, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org an updated proposed order on MC-053 with accurate information inserted, where appropriate. Note the Order will not be effective until Counsel files a Proof of Service of the executed order.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgement And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jan-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Robert Arthur Johnson's unopposed Motion To Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED.

"When service of a summons has not resulted in actual notice to a party in time to defend the action and a default or default judgment has been entered against him or her in the action, he or she may serve and file a notice of motion to set aside the default or default judgment and for leave to defend the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5(a).) "The notice of motion shall be served and filed within a reasonable time, but in no event exceeding the earlier of: (i) two years after entry of a default judgment against him or her; or (ii) 180 days after service on him or her of a written notice that the default or default judgment has been entered." (Ibid.) The motion "shall be accompanied by an affidavit showing under oath that the party's lack of actual notice in time to defend the action was not caused by his or her avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect." (Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5(b).) "The party shall serve and file with the notice a copy of the answer, motion, or other pleading proposed to be filed in the action." (Ibid.) The court may set aside the default or default judgment on a finding that the defendant's lack of actual notice was not due to his avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect. (Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5(c).) Unless inexcusable neglect is clear, the policy favoring trial on the merits prevails over the general rule of deference to the trial court's exercise of discretion, and doubts are resolved in favor of the application for relief from default. (Tunis v. Barrow (1986) 184 Cal.App.3d 1069,1079.)

Here, Defendant has satisfied the requirements of section 473.5. The court finds Defendant's lack of actual notice in time to defend the action was not caused by his or her avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect. Thus, the court sets aside the default and default judgment entered in this case.

The court grants Defendant leave to defend the action. Defendant's answer must be filed within ten court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default Judgment, Declaration
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: 8:44am. Prior to the hearing, Mr. Johnson appears in Dept. 302. The clerk instructs Mr. Johnson to read the court's tentative ruling.

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBERT JOHNSON's Motion To Vacate Default Judgment.

Defendant Robert Arthur Johnson's unopposed Motion To Vacate Default Judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Defendant's motion was neither timely filed nor timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail to place within State adds five calendar days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Basically, in order to be heard today, a motion served by mail to an address within the sate would have had to be filed and served no later than August 27 (16 court days plus 5 calendar days). Defendant, however, filed and served his motion on September 9, 2025---much too late. If Defendant files a new motion, he must comply with the requirements for timely filing and service.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
